misc.title: 🙏To all art fans around the world HELP😭 I'm Japanese traditional analog artist. I survive on commissions from clients in countries other than Japan. Recently, the situation on Twitter has worsened,
misc.text: 🙏To all art fans around the world HELP😭 I'm Japanese traditional analog artist. I survive on commissions from clients in countries other than Japan. Recently, the situation on Twitter has worsened, and the impressions on my posts have -90％plummeted... my traditional commissioned art life at risk...🥶 if you see this tweet, please commission me. 🤗 The commission pricing details will be included in the ALT section of this post. For more detailed pricing, please send me a direct message. Let's discuss it further. Thank you everyone.🙏🙏🙏

misc.title: As you can see, all of this could very well turn out to be true; the circumstantial evidence is quite strong, and the idea that the four gods had four gigantic beasts as allies makes perfect sense. I
misc.text: As you can see, all of this could very well turn out to be true; the circumstantial evidence is quite strong, and the idea that the four gods had four gigantic beasts as allies makes perfect sense. I also find the specific role assigned to each god to be very interesting. As mentioned above, they remind me of the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse. In *One Piece* in particular, themes such as destruction, famine, pestilence, death, war, and liberation are frequently explored. I also suspect that the Three Ancient Weapons are an allusion to or modeled after the powers of the four gods (or at least three of them); humanity sought to challenge the power of these deities and, through the use of science, created their own "gods." Ultimately, I believe that the portrayal of these gods and their partners makes perfect sense. What you guys think about this theory?

misc.title: And last but not least: Nika. In Nika's case, too, I believe he had a companion.And I suspect that his companion is currently trapped within the Florian Triangle. Furthermore, I think his partner migh
misc.text: And last but not least: Nika. In Nika's case, too, I believe he had a companion.And I suspect that his companion is currently trapped within the Florian Triangle. Furthermore, I think his partner might be a whale. This would simply fit *One Piece* perfectly; there are many indications that a whale could play an enormous role in the story. I believe that Laugh Tale exists inside the whale, and that this is precisely why the World Government has not yet been able to locate and destroy it because the whale protects it, even if it remains sealed away. The moment Luffy discovers the One Piece, he will also liberate the whale and find his partner.

misc.title: The next section: Rain God Zaza and Zunisha. Here, too, I feel that Zunisha is a perfect match for Zazaboth symbolically and in terms of its sheer size as a monster. I believe that Zaza is the reason
misc.text: The next section: Rain God Zaza and Zunisha. Here, too, I feel that Zunisha is a perfect match for Zazaboth symbolically and in terms of its sheer size as a monster. I believe that Zaza is the reason why Devil Fruit users cannot swim. We learned from the mural that the Sea God became enraged; I think Zaza's role is directly connected to the curse placed upon the Devil Fruits. Zunisha also completely flooded the entirety of Zou a perfect allusion to Zaza. Furthermore, since Zunisha embodies themes of betrayal and disloyalty, I think its role also aligns perfectly as a counterpart to Níðhöggr.

misc.title: Imu and Nidhogg: yes, I believe that Imu represents the Forest God, a being capable of creating "demons" and thereby controlling the world. This would also fit Imu perfectly, given the distinctly plan
misc.text: Imu and Nidhogg: yes, I believe that Imu represents the Forest God, a being capable of creating "demons" and thereby controlling the world. This would also fit Imu perfectly, given the distinctly plant-like atmosphere he has cultivated within his Flower Room. Furthermore, the Forest God could be the very reason why Devil Fruits were created in the first place. Nidhogg is not visible in the mural, as I suspect Franky is positioned in such a way that he completely blocks the view of him. In Norse mythology, Nidhogg is also situated at the base of the Yggdrasil tree (which aligns with Loki, who was bound at the roots of Yggdrasil). In the latest chapter, Imu mentions that Nidhogg is a traitor, Someone who (much like Zunesha, though I’ll save that for the next post) betrayed him during the Second World and sided with Joy Boy. Forest God > Imu Nidhogg > Loki

misc.title: First, there was the Earth God; he had the Hell Serpent as an ally, and together they set the world ablaze. The fact that the two share a connection seems self-evident to me; they were likely Nika's p
misc.text: First, there was the Earth God; he had the Hell Serpent as an ally, and together they set the world ablaze. The fact that the two share a connection seems self-evident to me; they were likely Nika's primary adversaries in the First World, most likely Davy Jones had the Earth God Power in the second world.

misc.title: Gente, el vídeo principal de esta semana en el canal, es un recopilatorio de todos los videojuegos existentes en la saga #TalesOf, reunificando para ello 2 vídeos especiales que hicimos hace 1 año, y
misc.text: Gente, el vídeo principal de esta semana en el canal, es un recopilatorio de todos los videojuegos existentes en la saga #TalesOf, reunificando para ello 2 vídeos especiales que hicimos hace 1 año, y rehaciendo completamente 3 especiales de hace ya bastante tiempo en el canal. Además de expandir y actualizar el conjunto con más contenido y datos que no mencionamos en su día. Esperamos poder tener listo el vídeo (que seguramente pasará de la hora y media) para mañana Domingo. Así que, sintiéndolo mucho, pasaremos de nuevo el Resumen Semanal para el Lunes. Como ya os comentamos en el canal, estamos a tope con el trabajo, y estas últimas 2 semanas (y seguramente 1 o 2 más), vamos haciendo lo que podemos para sacar los vídeos que teníamos planeados para adelante. Disculpad...

items.text: As you can see, all of this could very well turn out to be true; the circumstantial evidence is quite strong, and the idea that the four gods had four gigantic beasts as allies makes perfect sense. I also find the specific role assigned to each god to be very interesting. As mentioned above, they remind me of the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse. In *One Piece* in particular, themes such as destruction, famine, pestilence, death, war, and liberation are frequently explored. I also suspect that the Three Ancient Weapons are an allusion to or modeled after the powers of the four gods (or at least three of them); humanity sought to challenge the power of these deities and, through the use of science, created their own "gods." Ultimately, I believe that the portrayal of these gods and their partners makes perfect sense. What you guys think about this theory?

items.title: And last but not least: Nika. In Nika's case, too, I believe he had a companion.And I suspect that his companion is currently trapped within the Florian Triangle. Furthermore, I think his partner migh
items.text: And last but not least: Nika. In Nika's case, too, I believe he had a companion.And I suspect that his companion is currently trapped within the Florian Triangle. Furthermore, I think his partner might be a whale. This would simply fit *One Piece* perfectly; there are many indications that a whale could play an enormous role in the story. I believe that Laugh Tale exists inside the whale, and that this is precisely why the World Government has not yet been able to locate and destroy it because the whale protects it, even if it remains sealed away. The moment Luffy discovers the One Piece, he will also liberate the whale and find his partner.

items.text: The next section: Rain God Zaza and Zunisha. Here, too, I feel that Zunisha is a perfect match for Zazaboth symbolically and in terms of its sheer size as a monster. I believe that Zaza is the reason why Devil Fruit users cannot swim. We learned from the mural that the Sea God became enraged; I think Zaza's role is directly connected to the curse placed upon the Devil Fruits. Zunisha also completely flooded the entirety of Zou a perfect allusion to Zaza. Furthermore, since Zunisha embodies themes of betrayal and disloyalty, I think its role also aligns perfectly as a counterpart to Níðhöggr.

items.title: Imu and Nidhogg: yes, I believe that Imu represents the Forest God, a being capable of creating "demons" and thereby controlling the world. This would also fit Imu perfectly, given the distinctly plan
items.text: Imu and Nidhogg: yes, I believe that Imu represents the Forest God, a being capable of creating "demons" and thereby controlling the world. This would also fit Imu perfectly, given the distinctly plant-like atmosphere he has cultivated within his Flower Room. Furthermore, the Forest God could be the very reason why Devil Fruits were created in the first place. Nidhogg is not visible in the mural, as I suspect Franky is positioned in such a way that he completely blocks the view of him. In Norse mythology, Nidhogg is also situated at the base of the Yggdrasil tree (which aligns with Loki, who was bound at the roots of Yggdrasil). In the latest chapter, Imu mentions that Nidhogg is a traitor, Someone who (much like Zunesha, though I’ll save that for the next post) betrayed him during the Second World and sided with Joy Boy. Forest God > Imu Nidhogg > Loki

items.title: First, there was the Earth God; he had the Hell Serpent as an ally, and together they set the world ablaze. The fact that the two share a connection seems self-evident to me; they were likely Nika's p
items.text: First, there was the Earth God; he had the Hell Serpent as an ally, and together they set the world ablaze. The fact that the two share a connection seems self-evident to me; they were likely Nika's primary adversaries in the First World, most likely Davy Jones had the Earth God Power in the second world.
